rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:

Right lower lobe:
- Solid nodule measuring 7 mm along the interlobar fissure on image 119 (no change).

Left lower lobe:
- Solid subpleural nodule measuring 5 mm on image 118 (no change).

In both lungs, micronodules measure up to 3 mm (no change). In the left lower lobe, a calcified granuloma measures up to 6 mm, series 5, image 129. Few cysts in both lungs measure up to 1.1 cm (largest in the right lower lobe, series 5, image 142). In both lungs, mild ground-glass opacities are present (no change).

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Mild pericardial effusion measuring up to 6 mm in thickness. Coronary artery calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: Dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta measuring up to 4.0 cm, mild wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.2 cm.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. T2 vertebral hemangioma measuring up to 1.1 × 0.9 cm.

Included portions of the abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid nodules in both lower lobes and micronodules in both lungs. Per Fleischner Society 2017 recommendations, in high-risk patients, follow-up chest CT is recommended in 12 months; in low-risk patients, follow-up is not required.

2. Ground-glass opacities in both lungs may represent sequelae of prior inflammatory changes.

3. Calcified granuloma in the left lower lobe, consistent with prior infection.

4. Few cysts in both lungs.

5. Coronary artery calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Dilated ascending thoracic aorta (4.0 cm) with mild wall calcification.

7. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.2 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

8. T2 vertebral hemangioma.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Pulmonary nodules in series 5 are as follows:
Right lower lobe:
- Perifissural node measures 7 mm along the interlobar fissure on image 119 (no change) and requires no follow up.
Left upper lobe:
- Solid nodule measures 3 mm on image 73 (no change).
In the left lower lobe, a calcified granuloma measures up to 6 mm, series 5, image 129, requires no follow up. Few cysts in both lungs measure up to 1.1 cm (largest in the right lower lobe, series 5, image 142), clinically insignificant. In both lungs, mild ground-glass opacities are present (no change).

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy.

Heart: Cardiomegaly, cardiothoracic ratio is 0.57. Mild pericardial effusion measuring up to 6 mm in thickness, previously not seen. Coronary artery calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. 

Great vessels: Dilatation of the ascending thoracic aorta measuring up to 4.0 cm, moderate wall calcification. The right subclavian artery is dilated to 2.9 cm, unchanged. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.2 cm.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. T2 vertebral hemangioma measures up to 1.1 × 0.9 cm and requires no follow up.

Included portions of the abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. Aneurysmal dilation of the right subclavian artery up to 2.9 cm.

2. Perifissural node in Right lower lobe, Solid nodule in the Left upper lobe, calcified granuloma In the left lower lobe require no follow up. Ground-glass opacities in both lungs may represent sequelae of prior inflammatory changes.

3. Coronary artery calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Dilated ascending thoracic aorta (4.0 cm) with mild wall calcification.

5. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.2 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

6. Cardiomegaly. Mild pericardial effusion measuring up to 6 mm in thickness, previously not seen.

7. T2 vertebral hemangioma requires no follow up.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Mild areas of paraseptal emphysema. Single nodules are seen in the lung parenchyma, in series 4 as follows:
In the right lung:
— In the lower lobe:
* Nodule measuring up to 3 mm (image 126) - solid.
In the left lung:
— In the lower lobe:
* Subpleural nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 148) - subsolid.
Single calcifications are also seen in the lungs, the largest measuring up to 4 mm in the left upper lobe.
No pleural effusion. The costal pleura shows areas of localized thickening bilaterally up to 5 mm, with calcification (changes are more pronounced in the right upper lobe, and in the lower lobe on the left). Single air cysts.
The trachea and central bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: Increased size of intrathoracic lymph nodes of the prevascular group measuring up to 1.6 cm in short axis (series 3 image 48).

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Mitral valve replacement. Moderate coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall:
Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Deformity of the inferior endplate of the L1 vertebral body due to a Schmorl node measuring up to 1.1 cm.
Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to +114 HU (CT signs of osteopenia).

Upper abdomen:
Hypodense lesions in the liver parenchyma with well-defined smooth margins and fluid density measuring up to  mm.
In the mid third of the left renal parenchyma, two hypodense lesions with well-defined smooth margins and fluid density measure up to 1.8 × 1.5 cm and 2.0 × 1.9 cm, respectively.
Cholecystectomy.
In the lateral limb of the right adrenal gland, a fat-density lesion measures 8 × 7 mm.
Calcified lesion in the greater omentum measuring up to 12 × 8 mm - possibly sequelae of prior appendagitis/post-inflammatory process.

Thyroid gland: In the left lobe, a hypodense lesion measures up to 9 × 7 mm with somewhat ill-defined margins.
rad_impression_en:
IMPRESSION:
1. CT findings of single pulmonary nodules that do not require further follow-up per the 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations.

2. CT findings of paraseptal emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria. The current study may be used as a baseline for subsequent follow-up.

3. CT findings of intrathoracic lymphadenopathy (3A group).

4. Moderate coronary calcification.

5. Aortic valve calcification. Mitral valve replacement.

6. Thoracic aorta atherosclerosis.

7. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation, Schmorl node at L1. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis.

8. Hypodense renal lesions with fluid density: per “Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee” (2018), CT follow-up is not required.

9. Right adrenal lesion with a benign phenotype (likely myelolipoma): per “Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee” (2017), CT follow-up is not required.

10. Thyroid nodular changes: per “Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee” (2014), thyroid ultrasound is recommended.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Small areas of paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema are identified in the apices. A few calcified nodules are present in both lungs; the largest measures up to 4 mm in the left upper lobe, no follow-up is required. 
Solid nodules are seen in the lung parenchyma, in series 4, as follows:
In the right lower lobe:
- Nodule measures up to 2 mm (image 115), unchanged. 
- Nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 126), unchanged. 
In the left lower lobe:
- Subpleural nodule measures up to 3 mm (image 148), previously not seen. 
No pleural effusion. The costal pleura shows areas of localized thickening bilaterally up to 5 mm (unchanged), with calcification. An increase in the size of some calcifications is observed in time. 

Mediastinum and lung hila: Increased size of intrathoracic lymph nodes of the prevascular group with interval worsening. In series 3, an increase in the size of the lymph node on image 47 measures 1.5 cm (previously 4 mm), the lymph node on image 80 measures 1.2 cm (previously 4 mm); the lymph node on image 101 decreased to 1.1 cm (previously 1.5 cm).

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Mild aortic valve calcification. Mitral valve replacement. Moderate coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Deformity of the inferior endplate of the L1 vertebral body due to a Schmorl node measuring up to 1.1 cm. Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the T11-T12 level measures 114 HU (CT signs of demineralization). Severe degenerative changes of shoulder joints.

Upper abdomen: Hypodense lesion in the liver parenchyma in segment 1 with well-defined smooth margins and fluid density measures up to 9 mm, unchanged. In the left kidney, two hypodense lesions with well-defined smooth margins and fluid density measure up to 1.8 × 1.5 cm and 2.0 × 1.9 cm. Lesion identified in the upper pole is previously not seen. Cholecystectomy. The common bile duct measures up to 12 mm, unchanged. In the lateral limb of the right adrenal gland, a fat-density lesion measures 8 mm, requires no follow follow-up, unchanged.

Thyroid gland: In the left lobe, a hypodense lesion measures up to 9 × 7 mm.
val_impression_en:
IMPRESSION:
1. Compared to Chest CT on November 7, 2019, solid nodules in the right lower lobe are unchanged; a new solid nodule measures up to 3 mm in the left lower lobe. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: 
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for follow-up. 
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion.

2. CT findings of mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria. The current study may be used as a baseline for subsequent follow-up.

3. Multiple calcified pleural plaques with interval increase in the size of some calcifications may represent asbestos-related pleural disease.

4. Intrathoracic lymphadenopathy (3A group) with interval worsening compared to Chest CT on November 7, 2019, may be asbestos-related or related to neoplastic disease.

5. Moderate coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Mild aortic valve calcification. 

7. Left renal cysts. Per “Management of the Incidental Renal Mass on CT: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee” (2018), CT follow-up is not required.

8. Right adrenal lesion with a benign phenotype (likely myelolipoma), unchanged. Per “Management of Incidental Adrenal Masses: A White Paper of the ACR Incidental Findings Committee” (2017), an incidental adrenal mass that is <1 cm needs no further work-up or follow-up.

9. Left thyroid nodule. Per “Managing Incidental Thyroid Nodules Detected on Imaging: White Paper of the ACR Incidental Thyroid Findings Committee” (2014), no need for further follow-up.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:  
In both lungs: Pleuroapical fibrosis; areas of mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema; plate-like atelectasis in the anterior portions on the right and in lung bases on the left.  
In the left lung: Reticular changes in the posterior portions.  
In the right lung: Calcified nodule in the lower lobe measuring 5 mm on image 178.  
No pleural effusion.

Pulmonary solid nodules in series 6 are as follows:  
Right middle lobe:  
Nodule measuring 6 mm on image 160.

Mediastinum and hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Augmentation mammoplasty.

Included portions of the abdomen: Cholecystectomy. Three hypodense lesions in the liver in segments 4, 5, and 6, round, homogeneous, with indistinct margins, with maximum size 1.3 × 1.5 cm (segment 4). Two hypodense lesions in the body of the left adrenal gland, round, measuring 1.1 × 1.0 × 1.3 cm, density 4 HU, and up to 7 mm in diameter, density -46 HU. Along the posterior contour of the left kidney, a round hypodense lesion measuring up to 8 mm in diameter, density -23 HU. The left renal collecting system is dilated: renal pelvis up to 1.7 cm; no evidence of calyceal dilatation.
rad_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule up to 6 mm in the right middle lobe. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, CT follow-up in 12 months. Calcified pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe, not requiring follow-up.

2. Mild pulmonary emphysema.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as severe.

4. Cholecystectomy.

5. Hypodense lesion in the left kidney (likely a benign cyst; no further follow-up is required).

6. Hypodense liver lesions (likely benign cysts; no further follow-up is required).

7. Left pyelectasis (renal ultrasound is recommended).

8. Hypodense lesions in the right adrenal gland (CT follow-up in 12 months is recommended).
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral pleuroapical fibrosis. Mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema. Linear atelectasis in the middle lobe of the right lung and in the lung bases. In the dorsal portions of the left lung, reticular changes are present, possibly gravitational. Pulmonary nodules in series 6 are as follows:  
In the right lower lobe, calcified nodule measures 5 mm on image 178.  
In the right middle lobe, solid nodule measures 6 mm on image 160.
Multiple micronodules in both lungs, mostly subpleural and centrilobular, up to 1–2 mm (image 82, 87, 95, 99, 113, 154, 164).
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Multiple calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Bilateral breast implants.

Included portions of the abdomen: Cholecystectomy. Three hypodense lesions in the liver in segments 4, 5, and 6, round, homogeneous, with indistinct margins, with maximum size 1.3 × 1.5 cm (segment 4). Hypodense lesion in the body of the left adrenal gland measures 1.3 cm, density -1 HU. Along the posterior contour of the left kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 8 mm in diameter, density -23 HU. Sinus cyst measures up to 1.7 cm on the left.
val_impression_en:
1. Solid nodule measures 6 mm in the right middle lobe. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following: Initial follow-up chest CT at 6-12 months. If stable, another follow-up chest CT at 18-24 months. Calcified pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe, not requiring follow-up. Multiple micronodules in both lungs, mostly subpleural and centrilobular, may be inflammatory process/respiratory bronchiolitis.

2. Mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening.

3. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as severe. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta.

4. Cholecystectomy. Cysts in the left kidney, likely benign; no further follow-up is required. Hypodense liver lesions, likely benign cysts; no further follow-up is required.

5. Hypodense lesions in the right adrenal gland. ACR White Paper recommendations (Mayo-Smith, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2017;14:1038-1044) suggest the following: Benign adenoma. No need for further imaging follow-up. Consider biochemical evaluation to determine functional status.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Pleuroapical fibrosis in both lungs.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification is identified.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Single calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Unremarkable.

Included upper abdomen: In the upper segment and mid portions, calculi, 5 in number, measuring up to 2 × 2 × 2 mm. Hypodense round lesion in the upper segment of the left kidney, measuring up to 1.2 cm in diameter, density 8 HU. Right-sided Bochdalek hernia. Eventration of the left hemidiaphragm.
rad_impression_en:
1. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Bilateral pleuroapical fibrosis.

3. Mild calcification of the thoracic aorta.

4. Right-sided Bochdalek hernia. Eventration of the left hemidiaphragm.

5. Urolithiasis: Calculi in the left renal collecting system, without signs of obstruction.

6. Hypodense lesion of the left kidney (more likely a benign cyst, no further follow-up is required).
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral pleuroapical fibrosis. In the upper lobe of the left lung there is a 3 mm solid nodule (series 4, image 78), probably an intrapulmonary lymph node. No pleural effusion. 
Right-sided Bochdalek hernia. Relaxation of the left hemidiaphragm.

Mediastinum and hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Scattered calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Decrease Decreased vertebral body density: T11 - 105 HU, T12 - 123 HU, L1 - 106 HU, L2 - 111 HU.

Included upper abdomen: In the upper and middle segments of the left kidney, calculi, 5 in number, maximum size 2 mm. In the upper segment of the left kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 1.2 cm in diameter, density 8 HU.
val_impression_en:
1. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Left renal calculi, without signs of obstruction. Hypodense left renal lesion, more likely a benign cyst. No further follow-up is required.

3. Mild calcification of the thoracic aorta.

4. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura:  
No nodules requiring follow-up are identified.  
Against the background of bilateral, diffuse mosaic attenuation of the parenchyma, there is thickening of the walls of segmental and subsegmental bronchi (changes may be consistent with bronchitis).  
Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments on the left, and in lung bases bilaterally (clinically insignificant).  
No pleural effusion.  

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy are identified.  

Heart: Cardiac chambers are mildly enlarged. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Mild coronary artery calcification.  

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.  

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 135 HU, T12 - 123 HU, L1 - 112 HU.  

Upper abdomen: At the splenic hilum, there is a round lesion with well-defined margins, measuring up to 6 mm in diameter, likely an accessory splenule.
rad_impression_en:
1. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.  

2. Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation of the lung parenchyma with thickening of the walls of segmental and subsegmental bronchi; differentiate these changes from manifestations of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, hypersensitivity pneumonitis without fibrosis, and small airways disease (bronchiolitis).  

3. Findings of cardiomegaly.  

4. Thoracic aortic calcification.  

5. Mild coronary artery calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.  

6. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the lower lobe of the left lung there is a solid nodule measuring 7 mm (series 4, image 90). Nearby, a solid nodule is adjacent to the interlobar pleura and measures 4 mm (series 4, image 93). Bilateral, diffuse mosaic attenuation of the parenchyma with multiple air trapping. Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments on the left, and in lung bases bilaterally (clinically insignificant). No pleural effusion. Thickening of the walls of segmental and subsegmental bronchi (may be consistent with bronchitis). 

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy are identified.  

Heart: Cardiac chambers are mildly enlarged, cardiothoracic ratio 0.66. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left anterior descending and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.  

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 135 HU, T12 - 123 HU, L1 - 112 HU (CT signs of demineralization).

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. In the lower lobe of the left lung solid nodules measure up to 7 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, initial chest CT at 3-6 months. If stable, no further follow-up or an optional chest CT at 18-24 months if there is high index of suspicion.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, initial chest CT at 3-6 months. If stable, another chest CT at 18-24 months.

2. Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation of the lung parenchyma with multiple air trapping, and thickening of the walls of segmental and subsegmental bronchi represent bronchial disease (inflammatory or infectious) with bronchial obstruction. 

3. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left anterior descending and right coronary arteries, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Cardiomegaly. Thoracic aortic calcification.  

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
